mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer purchasing power, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance when unemployment remains above the threshold for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and forces equity prices higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns when retail sales and housing starts diverge, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during supply-constrained regimes indicates capacity bottlenecks and rising input costs, which compress corporate profit margins and trigger immediate short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive or flat despite falling industrial production, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored below 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 225.0 force financial markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when core inflation diverges from headline readings by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income and consumer spending, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns due to wage-driven inflation fears.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with declining retail sales confirms systemic demand destruction, which forces earnings downgrades and triggers rapid short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP stagnates but retail sales remain stable, or the interaction effect reverses direction during liquidity-driven market rallies.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices above 95.0 signal robust manufacturing activity and high capacity utilization, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when industrial production exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization falls below 75 percent despite high output.
